SUMMARY

This is AI generated summarization, which may have errors. For context, always refer to the full article.

MANILA, Philippines – After a one-day break, Team Philippines plunges back to action on Friday, August 2, with three Filipino bets looking to do the country proud.

Here is the schedule (Manila time):

Jarod Hatch exits the Paris Olympics after finishing tied for 36th out of 40 competitors in the heats of the men’s 100m butterfly with a time of 54.66 seconds.

Only the top 16 advance to the semifinals.

Hungary’s Kristof Milak, the silver medalist in the Tokyo Games, sets the pace with 50.19 seconds.

Read the full storyhere.

Joanie Delgaco posts her fastest time in the Paris Olympics as she finishes 20th overall in the women’s single sculls.

Delgaco clocks 7:43.83 to place second in the classification final D, giving it her all in the final 1,000 meters after crossing the first 1,000 meters at fifth place.

Read the full storyhere.

Aira Villegas celebrates her 29th birthday with the best possible present as she scores a unanimous decision win over Algeria’s Roumaysa Boualam to advance to the women’s 50kg quarterfinals.

Villegas clinches the first and third rounds to claim the 30-27, 30-27, 29-28, 29-28, 29-28 victory.

Facing France’s Wassila Lkhadiri in the last eight, Villegas is a win away from a guaranteed Olympic medal.

Read the full storyhere.

Boxer Aira Villegas aims to inch closer to a medal, swimmer Jarod Hatch opens his bid, while rower Joanie Delgaco hopes to end her campaign on a high note.

Villegas punches for aspot in the quarterfinalsof the women’s 50kg class when she faces second seed Roumaysa Boualam of Algeria in the round of 16.

Delgaco, meanwhile, races for the last time in the women’s single sculls as she vies for the 19th to 24th places in the final D.

Hatch competes in the heats of the men’s 100m butterfly, needing to be among the 16 fastest swimmers to advance to the semifinals.– Rappler.com

author: delfin.dioquino editor
